preview: Argentina's ball-dominant, pressing system under their current setup will test Switzerland's disciplined defensive structure and lightning-quick counterattacking through their midfield creators, with the match likely decided by whether Messi's successor in orchestrating play can break down a Swiss backline that thrives on organized compactness. The semifinal berth hanging in the balance transforms this into a chess match between Argentina's suffocating possession and Switzerland's lethal efficiency—a clash between controlling the tempo and exploiting the spaces it creates.
